reason: The only residual finding is a bracket notation mismatch (「」 vs 【】) in a file-save warning. While the literal text differs from source, the core instruction — delete these comment lines before saving — is fully preserved and users will be looking at actual JSP files where the 【説明】 comments are visually distinctive. The risk of a user failing to locate the correct lines due to this bracket discrepancy is low.

findings_assessment.justification: The knowledge file renders the warning as 「説明」で始まるコメント行 while the source uses 【説明】. A user following the AI's guidance would be directed to the correct action (delete those comment lines before saving) but with a slightly wrong description of which bracket style marks them. In practice, users work directly with the JSP file where the 【説明】 prefix is visually obvious, so the wrong bracket notation is unlikely to prevent them from finding and deleting the correct lines. The core intent and the required action are both accurately conveyed. Why Phase E did not resolve it: the Phase E output is byte-for-byte identical to the Phase D output — the fix was simply never applied to s4, meaning the improvement loop exited without actually correcting the bracket type.
